_notes: NBA scheduling constraints derived from the NBA CBA (2023), broadcast contracts, and arena operations.
The NBA uses Fastbreak.ai (since 2024) with 1M+ constraints; prior years used a custom MIP solver.
Key difference from EPL/NFL: back-to-back games are unavoidable but minimized; season density ~4x EPL.
Sources: NBA CBA 2023, ESPN/TNT/ABC/NBC/Amazon broadcast contracts, Fastbreak.ai public statements,
NBA Operations scheduling documentation, In-Season Tournament rules (2023-present).

hard.description: Each team plays exactly 82 regular-season games

hard.source: NBA scheduling formula — each of 4 division rivals plays home and away twice
hard.description: Each team plays 4 games against each division rival (2H + 2A) = 16 division games total

hard.distribution: 6 teams x4 games + 4 teams x3 games
hard.source: NBA scheduling formula — conference non-division opponents play 3 or 4 times
hard.description: Within the same conference, 10 non-division opponents: 6 play 4 times (2H+2A), 4 play 3 times (varies H/A), totaling 36 games

hard.source: NBA scheduling formula — all 15 opposite-conference teams play exactly twice
hard.description: Each team plays all 15 opposite-conference opponents exactly twice (1H + 1A) = 30 inter-conference games

hard.source: NBA CBA 2023 Art. XXII — player health and performance protection
hard.description: No team may play 4 games in any 5 consecutive nights

hard.source: NBA CBA 2023 Art. XXII — extended rest protection clause
hard.description: No team may play 8 games in any 12 consecutive nights

hard.source: NBA CBA 2023 — back-to-back games with travel exceeding 1000 miles to second venue are prohibited
hard.description: Back-to-back games where the second game requires travel of more than 1000 miles from the first venue are not permitted

hard.source: NBA CBA 2023 and league operations agreement — back-to-back limit for player welfare
hard.description: Each team should have no more than 16 back-to-back game pairs per season (target is 14)

hard.source: NBA operations — marquee broadcast events require rested, competitive teams
hard.description: Teams participating in Christmas Day, Opening Night, or IST Finals must have at least 3 days rest before the game

hard.source: NBA calendar — mandatory All-Star break; no regular season games scheduled
hard.description: No regular season games may be scheduled during the All-Star break weekend (Friday through Sunday)

hard.source: NBA All-Star operations — host arena unavailable in the days surrounding the All-Star game
hard.description: The All-Star host team cannot schedule home games in the 3 days before and 2 days after the All-Star break

hard.source: NBA In-Season Tournament rules (2023-present) — IST group stage games on designated Tuesdays and Fridays
hard.description: In-Season Tournament group stage games must be scheduled on league-designated Tuesday and Friday slots in November

hard.source: NBA operations — playoff seeding integrity; all 30 teams play on the last day of the regular season
hard.description: All 30 teams must be scheduled to play on the final day of the regular season to ensure fair playoff seeding

soft.source: NBA CBA / Fastbreak.ai optimization — primary welfare metric; league target is 14 B2Bs per team per season
soft.description: Minimize back-to-back game instances per team; target is ≤14 B2B pairs per team (was 18 pre-2023)

soft.source: NBA player welfare and union pressure — road B2Bs are most fatiguing; compound travel + game stress
soft.description: Avoid scheduling back-to-back games where both are road games (road B2B is harder than home B2B)

soft.source: NBA team operations — road trips beyond 6 games disrupt player routines and homelife
soft.description: Teams should not embark on road trips exceeding 6 consecutive away games

soft.source: NBA scheduling standard — each team plays 41H + 41A across the full season
soft.description: Each team plays exactly 41 home and 41 away games; the split should be roughly even (within 3 games) in each half of the season

soft.source: ABC/ESPN broadcast contract — Christmas Day is the highest-rated NBA broadcast day; near-contractual obligation
soft.description: Exactly 5 marquee games must be scheduled on Christmas Day; top-market teams must participate (near-hard constraint due to broadcast revenue)

soft.source: NBA/broadcast tradition — defending champion plays Opening Night for ring ceremony and marquee matchup
soft.description: The defending NBA champion must play on Opening Night (typically as home team for ring ceremony)

soft.source: NBA community relations — Martin Luther King Jr. Day preference for markets with historical significance
soft.description: Prefer scheduling home games on MLK Day for teams in markets with strong historical significance (Atlanta etc.)

soft.source: NBA Fastbreak.ai optimization — FTE factor measures rest advantage/disadvantage vs opponent; range -5 to +5 is acceptable
soft.description: The Fresh-Tired-Even (FTE) factor per game should stay within ±5; measures relative rest advantage (fresh=more rest, tired=less rest than opponent)

soft.source: NBA Fastbreak.ai / player welfare — transcontinental travel on a B2B second night severely impacts performance
soft.description: Minimize situations where teams cross more than 2 time zones on the second night of a back-to-back

soft.source: NBA arena operations — Friday and Saturday home games maximize ticket sales and arena revenue
soft.description: Prefer scheduling home games on Fridays and Saturdays to maximize local attendance and arena revenue

soft.source: NBA In-Season Tournament rules — IST group stage games should not be the second leg of a back-to-back
soft.description: Teams playing In-Season Tournament group stage games should not have played a game the night before

soft.source: NBA team operations — arena sharing with NHL, concerts, and other events creates hard calendar conflicts
soft.description: Home games must not be scheduled during arena-unavailability windows (NHL conflicts, major concerts, convention bookings) — treated as near-hard

soft.source: NBA/NFL broadcast awareness — Monday Night Football draws viewers away from NBA in NFL markets
soft.description: Avoid scheduling home games on Monday nights for teams in NFL markets (NY, LA, Chicago, Dallas, etc.) during the NFL regular season
